name: Burrito Egg Rolls
method: Take a skillet, and heat oil over the medium heat.
Put taco seasoning and beef in it—season with pepper and salt.
Drain fat from beef and put it back to skillet.
Put back beans and corn, and cook for 2 minutes.
Turn off the heat and take it out in a bowl.
Add shredded cheddar, green pepper, and tomato in it.
Put the mixture and the egg roll wrapper in the center. Roll it, and
continue this process with all burrito mixture and egg roll wrappers.
Take a skillet, and heat oil over the medium heat.
Fry each side of egg roll until it is golden brown.
Turn off the heat; drain the fat.
Repeat the process with other egg rolls.
In boiling water, dip cabbage leaves for 30 seconds. Pat them dry.
Take a skillet, and heat oil over the medium heat.
Put the onion in it and cook for 5 minutes.
Add garlic and ground beef. Cook for about 5 minutes.
Add taco seasoning mix, pepper, and salt.
Add cherry tomatoes, black beans, and corn.
Place cabbage leaves on a plate, and put beef mixture with cheese.
Fold cabbage leaves, then roll them.
Bake them until for around 10 minutes.
Take a skillet, and heat oil over the medium heat.
Put onion and cook until soft.
Put enchilada sauce and chicken in it.
Add lime juice; season with pepper and salt.
Put a tortilla on a board. Add beans and rice on top of it.
Put in chicken mixture, and top with both kinds of cheese.
Roll burrito and put in a dish.
Put enchilada sauce on burritos; sprinkle extra cheeses.
Add cilantro; serve with hot sauce and sour cream.
Take a bowl, and mix eggs, chives, and milk; season with pepper and
Take a skillet, and melt butter in it.
Add the egg mixture in it. Cook each side for almost 2 minutes.
Place it on a plate; top with black beans, bacon, avocado, salsa, and
Take a skillet, and heat oil over the medium heat.
Put the onion in it and cook for 5 minutes.
Put ground beef and cook for around 6 minutes.
Add spices, and season with pepper and salt.
Put avocado on the serving plate.
Put cheese slices between cuts, and top with beef mixture.
